perturbation.original_text: 4.1.4 Maintenance of the Rail Facility. Subject to the terms, conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, Owner hereby authorizes and empowers Operator, and Operator agrees, in the name of and on behalf of Owner, to, at its sole cost and expense, keep and maintain the Rail Facility in a condition and repair similar to, but not less than, its condition and repair on the Effective Date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.4 Maintenance of the Rail Facility. Subject to the terms, conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, Owner hereby authorizes and empowers Operator, and Operator agrees, in the name of and on behalf of Owner, to, at Owner's sole cost and expense, keep and maintain the Rail Facility in a condition and repair similar to, but not less than, its condition and repair on the Effective Date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding who bears the costs of maintaining the Rail Facility. Originally, the Operator was responsible for the maintenance costs. However, the modified text now states that the Owner bears these costs. This contradicts the later section 4.1.10, which implies the Operator initially pays these costs. Section 4.1.11 also expects the operator to administer the budget.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.10 Payment of Operating Expenses. Operator shall promptly pay all direct costs and expenses incurred in operating and maintaining the Rail Facility as they become due, without reimbursement by Owner save and except to the extent specifically provided otherwise herein.
perturbation.justification: The modified text shifts the financial responsibility for maintaining the Rail Facility from the Operator to the Owner. This directly contradicts section 4.1.10, which states, 'Operator shall promptly pay all direct costs and expenses incurred in operating and maintaining the Rail Facility...' The change in cost allocation creates a clear and direct financial contradiction within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.11 Operating Expense Budget. Operator shall (i) administer and otherwise comply with the budget proposed by Operator and approved by Owner (ii) operate and maintain the Rail Facility in compliance with the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget established for the Rail Facility; provided, however, if Owner instructs Operator to perform any services outside of the scope of the services contemplated in the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget (exclusive of those services to be performed by Operator in connection with any necessary alterations or modifications to the Containment Pan System pursuant to Section 4.1.1), then Owner shall reimburse Operator for the costs and expenses associated therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.11 Operating Expense Budget. Operator shall (i) administer and otherwise comply with the budget proposed by Owner and approved by Operator (ii) operate and maintain the Rail Facility in compliance with the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget established for the Rail Facility; provided, however, if Owner instructs Operator to perform any services outside of the scope of the services contemplated in the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget (exclusive of those services to be performed by Operator in connection with any necessary alterations or modifications to the Containment Pan System pursuant to Section 4.1.1), then Owner shall reimburse Operator for the costs and expenses associated therewith.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the responsibility of proposing a Budget from Operator to Owner, introducing contradiction. The text previously stated the 'Operator' proposes the budget which the 'Owner' approves, where as the changed version has it as the 'Owner' proposes the budget, which the 'Operator' approves. This creates uncertainty on who has the responsibility of proposing the budget.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.11 Operating Expense Budget. Operator shall (i) administer and otherwise comply with the budget proposed by Owner and approved by Operator (ii) operate and maintain the Rail Facility in compliance with the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget established for the Rail Facility; provided, however, if Owner instructs Operator to perform any services outside of the scope of the services contemplated in the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget (exclusive of those services to be performed by Operator in connection with any necessary alterations or modifications to the Containment Pan System pursuant to Section 4.1.1), then Owner shall reimburse Operator for the costs and expenses associated therewith.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the responsibility of proposing the budget from the Operator to the Owner, while the contradicted text reflects this change. This introduces a contradiction because both parties cannot be simultaneously responsible for proposing the same budget. The original text assigning the operator to create the budget is directly contradicted by the change, and the contradicted text reinforces this contradiction as both sections read the same. This creates an ambiguity and thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Operator Default. Owner may terminate this Agreement at any time upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) the dissolution or bankruptcy of Operator; (b) Operator fails to pay when due any amount due and payable hereunder, including, without limitation, any interest, fees, reimbursements or indemnifications, and such failure shall continue for a period of 15 days after the due date thereof; (c) other than as set forth in clause (b) above, Operator breaches or fails to observe or perform any material term, condition, or obligation contained in this Agreement and fails to correct, or fails to diligently pursue correction of, such breach within 60 days after receipt of written notice from Owner of any such breach; (d) Termination by Owner pursuant to Section 4.1.1; or (e) Without cause on no less than seven (7) days prior written notice. In the event Owner terminates this Agreement pursuant to subparagraph (e) above, Owner may reimburse Operator for any unamortized capital expenditure (determined in accordance with GAAP) to the extent such capital expenditure was included in the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget. If any of the above occurs, other than an event described in subparagraph (d), and Owner elects to terminate this Agreement (a termination for "cause"), then Owner may give a written notice of termination to Operator, which termination shall be effective on the date specified by Owner in the notice, provided that such termination date shall be within 60 days of the date the notice is delivered to Operator. Owner's notice of breach to Operator under Section 3.3(b) or (c) shall state with particularity the breach alleged by Owner. To the extent Operator disputes the basis for Owner's notice of breach, the matter shall be addressed under Section 8.6. Nothing in this Section 3.3 shall be construed to limit or preclude any remedy Owner may have at law or in equity with respect to any material breach by Operator.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Operator Default. Owner may terminate this Agreement at any time upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) the dissolution or bankruptcy of Operator; (b) Operator fails to pay when due any amount due and payable hereunder, including, without limitation, any interest, fees, reimbursements or indemnifications, and such failure shall continue for a period of 15 days after the due date thereof; (c) other than as set forth in clause (b) above, Operator breaches or fails to observe or perform any material term, condition, or obligation contained in this Agreement and fails to correct, or fails to diligently pursue correction of, such breach within 60 days after receipt of written notice from Owner of any such breach; (d) Termination by Owner pursuant to Section 4.1.1; or (e) With cause on no less than seven (7) days prior written notice. In the event Owner terminates this Agreement pursuant to subparagraph (e) above, Owner may reimburse Operator for any unamortized capital expenditure (determined in accordance with GAAP) to the extent such capital expenditure was included in the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget. If any of the above occurs, other than an event described in subparagraph (d), and Owner elects to terminate this Agreement (a termination for "cause"), then Owner may give a written notice of termination to Operator, which termination shall be effective on the date specified by Owner in the notice, provided that such termination date shall be within 60 days of the date the notice is delivered to Operator. Owner's notice of breach to Operator under Section 3.3(b) or (c) shall state with particularity the breach alleged by Owner. To the extent Operator disputes the basis for Owner's notice of breach, the matter shall be addressed under Section 8.6. Nothing in this Section 3.3 shall be construed to limit or preclude any remedy Owner may have at law or in equity with respect to any material breach by Operator.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces a conflict in the termination clause, specifically section 3.3. The original contract allows termination "Without cause on no less than seven (7) days prior written notice". The changed version of the contract enforces that the contract can be terminated "With cause on no less than seven (7) days prior written notice.", now it has a pre-requisite of "Cause" for the termination to take place, contradicting the previous statement in the original contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Operator Default. Owner may terminate this Agreement at any time upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) the dissolution or bankruptcy of Operator; (b) Operator fails to pay when due any amount due and payable hereunder, including, without limitation, any interest, fees, reimbursements or indemnifications, and such failure shall continue for a period of 15 days after the due date thereof; (c) other than as set forth in clause (b) above, Operator breaches or fails to observe or perform any material term, condition, or obligation contained in this Agreement and fails to correct, or fails to diligently pursue correction of, such breach within 60 days after receipt of written notice from Owner of any such breach; (d) Termination by Owner pursuant to Section 4.1.1; or (e) With cause on no less than seven (7) days prior written notice. In the event Owner terminates this Agreement pursuant to subparagraph (e) above, Owner may reimburse Operator for any unamortized capital expenditure (determined in accordance with GAAP) to the extent such capital expenditure was included in the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget. If any of the above occurs, other than an event described in subparagraph (d), and Owner elects to terminate this Agreement (a termination for "cause"), then Owner may give a written notice of termination to Operator, which termination shall be effective on the date specified by Owner in the notice, provided that such termination date shall be within 60 days of the date the notice is delivered to Operator. Owner's notice of breach to Operator under Section 3.3(b) or (c) shall state with particularity the breach alleged by Owner. To the extent Operator disputes the basis for Owner's notice of breach, the matter shall be addressed under Section 8.6. Nothing in this Section 3.3 shall be construed to limit or preclude any remedy Owner may have at law or in equity with respect to any material breach by Operator.
perturbation.justification: The change modifies section 3.3(e) from "without cause" to "with cause." This creates a direct contradiction within the same section (3.3) because the original section 3.3 stated that termination could be done *without* cause, but the changed text implies it can only be done *with* cause. This introduces an inconsistency regarding the grounds for termination and conflicts with the original meaning of the text.
